title: Trump and Xi Enter China Summit Distracted by Iran War
summary: The war in Iran has cast a shadow of uncertainty on both superpowers, dimming early hopes that they could begin to address the larger issues that have frayed their relationship.

title: Hegseth to Accompany Trump on Trip to China
summary: Defense secretaries have previously traveled with presidents on overseas trips, to provide advice and represent the U.S. military.

title: Pentagon Puts Iran War Cost at $29 Billion as Hegseth Deflects on Funding
summary: The estimate from the Defense Department’s top budget official was $4 billion higher than two weeks ago. Secretary Pete Hegseth declined to say when the administration would request extra money for the Iran conflict.

title: Kuwait Accuses Iran of Trying to Infiltrate Its Territory
summary: Kuwait’s interior ministry said a group of armed Iranians arrived at Bubiyan Island in the Persian Gulf on May 1 aboard a rented fishing boat and exchanged fire with Kuwaiti soldiers.

title: The Iran War Is Taking the Color Out of Japan’s Best-Known Snack Bags
summary: The food giant Calbee said shortages of naphtha, a crude-oil derivative used in inks, were forcing it to switch to black-and-white packaging for its salty products.

title: CPI Shows Inflation Accelerating to 3.8% Annually in April After Weeks of War in Iran
summary: The Consumer Price Index rose 3.8% in April from a year earlier as higher energy costs replaced tariffs as the driver of higher prices for Americans.

title: Marty Makary, Trump’s F.D.A. Commissioner, Resigns After Weeks of Pressure
summary: The agency’s top food official will step in to the role after Dr. Makary privately said he opposed the administration decision to approve flavored vapes.

title: Reflecting Pool Repairs Appear Uneven and Behind Schedule, Officials Say
summary: Work on the iconic National Mall basin appears behind schedule. President Trump initially said he had picked the contractor, then said he did not know it.

title: How to Eat Through Our List of the Best Restaurants in New York City
summary: Don’t have the time (or money) to try all 100? Here are a dozen bite-size bucket lists to inspire you.

title: How an ‘Impossible’ Idea Led to a Pancreatic Cancer Breakthrough
summary: The new strategy also holds promise for lung and colon tumors. Here’s how scientists discovered it.

title: More Cancer Patients Are Taking Ivermectin. Mel Gibson and Joe Rogan Might Be Why.
summary: Prescriptions for antiparasitic drugs spiked after the actor claimed it cured his friends of cancer, a new study finds.

title: Is Anthropic’s Claude Mythos Really a Cybersecurity Risk?
summary: Anthropic said that Claude Mythos was too dangerous to release to the public. That claim has reopened an old debate over cybersecurity.

title: A.I. and Humans Battle It Out in a Cybersecurity Showdown
summary: Experts and college students used A.I. agents to try to break into and defend computer networks in a national competition. The agents did all right on their own, too.

title: Why So Many Guys Are Obsessed With Testosterone
summary: From the Trump administration to online influencers, the hormone is increasingly seen as the key to achieving a new male ideal.

title: What to Know About the New Obsession With Testosterone
summary: From politics to influencers and beyond, the hormone is being used not just for medical reasons but in pursuit of a new masculine ideal.

title: How New York City Is Solving Its Budget Crisis
summary: A generous influx of state resources from Gov. Kathy Hochul and a new tax on luxury second homes will help Mayor Zohran Mamdani address a projected $5.4 billion deficit.

title: Mysterious Meddling in Democratic Primaries Has G.O.P. Fingerprints
summary: A new super PAC with ties to Republicans is intervening in Democratic primaries, in one instance helping a sex therapist in Texas accused of antisemitism.

title: Alabama Governor Sets New Primary Elections for Four House Seats
summary: Gov. Kay Ivey scheduled special House primaries for August after the Supreme Court cleared a path for Alabama to use a 2023 congressional map with one majority-Black district.

title: Raffensperger Campaign Event at Georgia Airport Is Disrupted by ‘Active Threat,’ Bomb Squad on Site
summary: A suspicious object that disrupted a campaign stop for Brad Raffensperger, the Republican secretary of state in Georgia and a candidate for governor, was not a bomb, officials said.

title: Inside the Hantavirus Quarantine Unit in Nebraska Where 15 Americans Wait
summary: The National Quarantine Unit in Nebraska is now the temporary home for many of the Americans who had been passengers on the MV Hondius.

title: In Cleveland, Smokers Are Helping to Keep the Arts Alive
summary: A novel cigarette tax has generated $270 million for cultural organizations in the Cleveland area, which makes declining smoking rates “a double-edged sword.”

title: YouTube Plays Matchmaker for Sponsors and Stars
summary: The company is trying to help streaming influencers, who are increasingly being wooed by competitors like Netflix and TikTok.

title: Why Your Life Is Now on Subscriptions
summary: As companies look to build cash flow and loyalty, everything from heated car seats to earthworm deliveries can become a recurring charge on your credit card.

title: Why Young People Are Breaking Into Scientology Churches for TikTok
summary: The police in Los Angeles, New York, Sydney and elsewhere are responding to incidents related to a TikTok trend involving running through Scientology buildings.

title: Hacky Sack Mounts a Comeback With Gen Z
summary: Teenagers are booting the game out of the 1990s. “It’s kind of bringing everybody together,” one said.

title: Oregon Prepares for a Challenging Summer of Water Shortages and High Fire Risk
summary: After a warm winter that brought more rain than snow, the state’s snowpack was the lowest it has ever been.

title: Wildfires Strike Florida, Georgia and America’s ‘Wood Basket’
summary: Large fires, fueled by a record breaking drought, have been destroying homes and timber plantations in Southeastern states.

title: China Is Much Weaker Than It Seems. That’s the Problem.
summary: History shows there is no more productive national asset than freedom.

title: I Have Some Questions for the Democrats Who Want to Run California
summary: The Opinion columnist Ezra Klein moderates a forum with five of the top Democratic candidates for governor of California.

title: Person Fatally Struck by Frontier Airlines Jet at Denver Airport Is Identified
summary: The individual, a 41-year-old man, breached a fence at the airport on Friday minutes before being struck by a Frontier Airlines jet, officials said.

title: Justice Department Charges Companies in Baltimore Bridge Collapse
summary: The criminal charges are the latest legal fallout after the 2024 crash, which killed six men who were working on the Key Bridge.

title: Student Is Fatally Stabbed in University of Washington Housing Complex
summary: Police officers responding to a call found the student dead in the laundry room of an off-campus residential building. Investigators are now searching for a suspect.
